name: Dehesas de Alconchel Trail
name_original: Dehesas de Alconchel – Ruta BTT 5 Centro BTT Alqueva (IMBA)
description: This difficult 36.5-mile mountain biking route in Extremadura offers historical landmarks and scenic views of the Dehesas landscape.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Alconchel offers diverse terrain in the southwest of the Badajoz province, Extremadura, Spain. The region features typical pastureland with flat meadows and is defined by its proximity to the Great Lake of Alqueva. Mountain bike trails often traverse various mountain ranges, including the Sierra de Alor and the Sierra de Montehongo, providing varied landscapes for riders. Natural waterways like the Alcarrache river and the Friegamuñoz stream further enhance the scenic beauty. Best mountain bike trails around Alconchel The most popular mountain bike trail is Dehesas de Alconchel Trail, a 36.5 miles (58.8 km) difficult trail that takes about 5 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route traverses varied terrain, including pastureland. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embalse de Alqueva y Playa de Cheles – ida y vuelta desde Alconchel, a moderate 33.9 miles (54.6 km) path. This trail leads towards the Great Lake of Alqueva and the Cheles beach area. Local mountain bikers also love the Playa de Cheles desde Cheles — circular por Ruta 4 centro BTT Alqueva (IMBA), a 10.5 miles (16.9 km) trail leading through the Alqueva Lake area, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Mountain biking around Alconchel is defined by typical pastureland, the Great Lake of Alqueva, and mountain ranges like the Sierra de Alor. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Alconchel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alconchel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Alconchel?
A: There are 12 mountain bike trails around Alconchel, catering to various skill levels. These are part of the larger Alqueva Lake MTB Center, which offers over 300 kilometers of routes.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Alconchel?
A: The terrain around Alconchel is diverse, featuring typical pastureland with flat meadows, varied mountain ranges like the Sierra de Alor and Sierra de Montehongo, and scenic routes along natural waterways such as the Alcarrache river and Friegamuñoz stream. You'll also find trails near the Great Lake of Alqueva.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Alconchel?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy mountain bike trail available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351340367/playa-de-cheles-desde-cheles-circular-por-ruta-4-centro-btt-alqueva-imba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Cheles desde Cheles — circular por Ruta 4 centro BTT Alqueva (IMBA)&lt;/a&gt; is a 16.9 km route that takes about 1 hour 19 minutes to complete.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Alconchel?
A: You can explore several historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5529327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Miraflores&lt;/a&gt;, an Arab-origin castle, offers panoramic views. Other notable features include the ruins of the 16th-century Convento de la Luz, ancient aqueduct remains, and the medieval Bogaña Bridge over the Alcarrache river.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Alconchel?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3128829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alor Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent vistas, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5529327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Miraflores&lt;/a&gt; also offers impressive panoramic views from its elevated position overlooking Alconchel.

Q: What natural features can I expect to encounter on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often lead through typical pastureland, offering opportunities to observe rich fauna. You'll also ride alongside natural waterways like the Alcarrache river and Friegamuñoz stream, and enjoy stunning views of the Great Lake of Alqueva, Europe's largest artificial lake.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Alconchel?
A: Yes, there are circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1351340367/playa-de-cheles-desde-cheles-circular-por-ruta-4-centro-btt-alqueva-imba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Cheles desde Cheles — circular por Ruta 4 centro BTT Alqueva (IMBA)&lt;/a&gt; is a circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1352978142/castillo-de-alconchel-from-san-benito-de-la-contienda-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Alconchel from San Benito de la Contienda — circular&lt;/a&gt; also offers a loop experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Alconchel?
A: The mountain bike trails around Alconchel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the scenic beauty of the region, including the pastureland and views of the Great Lake of Alqueva.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, there are 3 difficult trails for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031578039/dehesas-de-alconchel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas de Alconchel Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 58.8 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1353014024/guadiana-river-nature-trail-alconchel-and-cheles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana River Nature Trail: Alconchel and Cheles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option spanning 62.8 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Alconchel?
A: The region experiences hot summers, so spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures and comfortable riding conditions. Winter can also be suitable, though some routes might be affected by rain.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to the Great Lake of Alqueva or a beach?
A: Yes, several routes offer access to the Great Lake of Alqueva. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031546272/embalse-de-alqueva-y-playa-de-cheles-ida-y-vuelta-desde-alconchel-ruta-3-centro-btt-alqueva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alqueva y Playa de Cheles – ida y vuelta desde Alconchel&lt;/a&gt; trail specifically leads towards the lake and the Cheles beach area, which is known for its blue flag designation.

Q: What other attractions are near the mountain bike trails in Alconchel?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several nearby attractions. These include prehistoric megalithic sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3909927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cromlech of Xerez&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7243420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menir da Bulhoa&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby municipality of Cheles also features historic buildings and a river beach.
tips: In a spectacular setting, we come across this Franciscan convent, almost 400 years old (15th century). It is in a state of ruin. Next to it, we can see the aqueduct (18th century), which served to supply water to the building. A place to visit, preferably in spring or autumn.
Hidden between roads and extensive Extremaduran meadows, we find the beautiful Bogaña bridge that allows us to cross the Alcarrache river.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike the Dehesas de Alconchel – Ruta BTT 5?
faqs.answer: This challenging mountain biking route typically takes around 5 hours and 6 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate for experienced riders, as the trail is classified as difficult.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The Dehesas de Alconchel – Ruta BTT 5 is considered a difficult route, designated as a 'Red' level trail within the Espacio BTT Alqueva network. It is best suited for experienced riders in good physical condition due to its technical elements and demanding terrain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied and challenging terrain, including sections with rocks, roots, and potentially steep descents. The route winds through the characteristic 'Dehesas' landscape of open woodlands with scattered oak trees, offering picturesque views of the artificial Lake Alqueva.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Dehesas de Alconchel – Ruta BTT 5?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical landmarks. You'll encounter the unique 'Dehesas' landscape and views of Lake Alqueva. Notable historical features include the 16th-century Los Jarales aqueduct-bridge, which once supplied water to the Convent of La Luz, and the Convent and Aqueduct of Nuestra Señora de la Luz, as well as the Puente de la Bogaña. There are also mentions of 'mysterious caves' in the area.

faqs.question: Is the Dehesas de Alconchel – Ruta BTT 5 dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular route are not detailed, it is generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash when mountain biking in natural areas, especially those with wildlife or livestock. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and can handle the route's difficult terrain and length.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: The Espacio BTT Alqueva network, which includes Ruta BTT 5, was developed to promote sports tourism. Generally, access to these homologated BTT routes in Spain does not require a specific permit or entrance fee for individual use. However, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations or event-specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this trail is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for strenuous activity. Summers can be very hot in Extremadura, making the difficult route particularly challenging, while winters can be cooler and potentially wet.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The Dehesas de Alconchel – Ruta BTT 5 starts in the town of Alconchel. You should be able to find suitable parking within or near Alconchel, as it serves as a starting point for this and other routes in the Centro BTT Alqueva network. Look for designated parking areas for visitors or cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there other mountain biking routes in the Centro BTT Alqueva network?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Dehesas de Alconchel – Ruta BTT 5 is part of the larger Espacio BTT Alqueva network, which spans over 300 kilometers and includes seven distinct routes. This network, homologated by IMBA, offers a variety of trails ranging in difficulty, with Ruta 5 being the most challenging.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino Natural del Guadiana, covering approximately 25% of its length. This allows for a diverse riding experience, combining dedicated BTT trails with parts of a recognized natural path.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts in Alconchel, you can find basic amenities such as cafes and potentially local accommodation options there. For a wider range of services, including more cafes, pubs, and accommodation suitable for cyclists, you might consider nearby towns like Villanueva del Fresno.

waypoints.name: Convent and Aqueduct of Nuestra Señora de la Luz
